getGenesForCreatureParts_twoNonSeparatingHulls copied from _singleNonSeparatingHull: same GeneDescription().separation(false).nodes({NodeDescription().cellTypeData(ConstructorGenomeDescription().geneIndex(N))}) builder blocks, edited geneIndex/separation/expected vector

false_positives.what_happened: ShapeGenerator dispatch is a family of generateNextConstructionDataForSegment/Triangle/Rectangle/Hexagon/Tube/Lolli/Zigzag functions plus getPreferredFrontAngle, each built from the same repeated assignment idiom (result.numAdditionalConnections=...; result.requiredNodeId[i]=...; result.requiredNodeAngle1[i]=...; result.requiredNodeAngle2[i]=...) and parallel switch(shape){case ...: return literal;} blocks. The cross-file hit ShapeGenerator.h:67-87 <- ShapeGenerator.cpp:334-355 (w=0.60 line=0.32) matches two such switch/return literal tables that share structure but encode different geometry constants.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: treat blocks dominated by a single repeated statement template (>=70% of lines normalize to one assignment/return shape with only array-index/literal differences) as a low-information idiom and suppress; for switch-on-enum returning literals, require token-identity not just structural-shape before reporting. Combine with the existing line-similarity floor (line=0.32 is far below any sane threshold and should never surface).

false_positives.what_happened: WIP rewrite that adds InspectionWindow.cpp (+879) while deleting InspectorWindow.cpp (-901). The new file is overwhelmingly a renamed copy of the deleted one (InspectorWindow->InspectionWindow). A naive cross-file detector flags this as massive duplication, but it is a file move/rename, not parallel duplicated code (the source no longer exists).
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056/diff-mode: extend rename detection (already added in 0beb2f7) to handle add+delete pairs within one commit where similarity is very high AND the basename differs by a stable substring (Inspector->Inspection). Score the pair as a rename, exclude both sides from copy-paste accounting.

false_positives.what_happened: (de)serialization is a long sequence of ParameterParser::encodeDecode(tree, field, default, ".Name", parserTask) calls, one per field. Consecutive lines are near-identical, only field/default/name change. Token n-gram matching sees high overlap between any two windows of these call runs. CORPUS_CHECK_DETAIL already flagged SerializerService.cpp:225-244 <- 122-141 (w=0.97 line=0.50) from this idiom.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: add a 'serialization/registration run' guard - if a candidate block is a contiguous run of calls to the SAME function name (>=N identical callee, varying only argument literals), down-weight or suppress as a builder/registration idiom rather than copied logic.
